exams.title: DEADLOCK
exams.description: Closed questions covering the titles, subtitles, and facts found in this Markdown source.

questions.prompt: What must you memorize about Definition: A resource?
choices: Definition: A resource is any physical or logical entity that must be acquired, used, and released over time (e.g., printers, scanners, database records, semaphores).
Preemptable: Can be taken away from a process with no ill effects (e.g., CPU, memory). Taking away memory via swapping usually doesn't cause failure.
Nonpreemptable: Cannot be taken away without causing failure (e.g., a printer in the middle of printing, a 3D scanner). Deadlocks only involve nonpreemptable resources.
The Problem: If processes acquire resources in a different order, a deadlock can occur (e.g., Process A holds R1 and waits for R2, while Process B holds R2 and waits for R1). ![ref1]
questions.explanation: Correct: Definition: A resource is any physical or logical entity that must be acquired, used, and released over time (e.g., printers, scanners, database records, semaphores).

choices: In 1988, Microsoft decided to develop a “new technology (NT) portable operating system that supported both the OS/2 and POSIX APIs.

environment but during development NT was changed to use the Win32 API, reflecting the popularity of Windows 3.0. ● Many versions of Windows along the way, XP, Vista, 7, 8, 10, and now
Many older versions still in use, less secure, some unpatched.

questions.prompt: What must you memorize about Windows?
choices: Originally, NT was supposed to use the OS/2 API as its native
environment but during development NT was changed to use the Win32 API, reflecting the popularity of Windows 3.0. ● Many versions of Windows along the way, XP, Vista, 7, 8, 10, and now
Windows uses the Win32 subsystem as the main operating environment; Win32 is used to start all processes. • It also provides all the keyboard, mouse and graphical display capabilities.
